callow.def: 羽毛未豐的、不成熟的、無經驗地 (不成熟)
callow.ex: He was just a callow youth when he started his first job.

callus.def: (醫)老繭、骨痂、起繭的人的人 (或物) (繭)
callus.ex: The guitarist has a thick callus on each of his fingertips.

calm.def: 平靜的、鎮定的、平靜、使安靜、安靜的人的人、安靜地 (靜)
calm.col: keep calm, calm down, calm before the storm (暴風雨前的寧靜)
calm.ex: Please try to stay calm and tell me what happened.

calmly.ex: She explained the entire situation to him very calmly.

calmness.ex: He handled the emergency with remarkable calmness.

caloric/calorific.def: 熱量的、產生熱的的人物 (熱)
caloric/calorific.ex: We should monitor our daily caloric intake at the gym.

calorie/calory.ex: Walking is an excellent way to burn extra calories.

calumniate.def: 誹謗、中傷的人 (或物)、誹謗地 (誹)
calumniate.ex: The senator refused to calumniate his political opponents.

calumny.ex: He felt that he had been the victim of a cruel calumny.

calvary.def: 受難像、連續的痛苦的人的人 (單數)
calvary.ex: He described his long illness as his personal calvary.

calve.def: 產犢、(冰川)崩解、崩解的人的人 (或物) (產犢)
calve.ex: The farmers watched as the prize cow began to calve.

calvinism.def: 加爾文教派、預定論 (單數)
calvinism.ex: His grandfather was a man of strict and somber calvinism.

calving.def: 產犢、冰川崩解的人 (或物) (崩)
calving.ex: The calving of the glacier was a spectacular sight to see.

calvity.ex: He was concerned about his premature calvity in his late 20s.

calypso.def: 卡里普索(西印度群島歌謠) (單數)
calypso.ex: The lively calypso beat kept everyone dancing all night.

calyx.ex: The green calyx protects the flower while it is in bud.

cam.def: 凸輪、(俚)攝影機節錄的人物 (凸輪)
cam.ex: The mechanic checked the cam for any signs of wear.

cambric.def: 白麻布、細棉布、細棉地 (布)
cambric.ex: The wedding dress was made of the finest white cambric.

cambridge.def: 劍橋、藍色的物、劍橋的人的人 (英)
cambridge.ex: He graduated with honors from Cambridge University.

camel.def: 駱駝、駝色、像駱駝的人的人、駝色地 (駱駝)
camel.ex: A camel can travel for days across the desert without water.

camellia.ex: The garden was filled with the scent of pink camellias.

cameo.def: 浮雕珠寶、(影)客串演出、名伶的人的人 (客串)
cameo.ex: The famous director made a brief cameo appearance in the film.

camera.def: 照相機、攝影機、在攝影機前的人物 (單數)
camera.col: digital camera, in camera (私下/秘密地), camera lens
camera.ex: He always carries a digital camera in his travel bag.

cameraman.ex: The cameraman captured the action with great skill.

camisole.ex: She wore a black silk camisole under her white suit.

camomil/chamomile.ex: Camomile tea is very helpful for a good night's sleep.

camouflage.def: 偽裝、掩飾、隱蔽、偽裝的人的人、隱蔽地 (偽裝)
camouflage.ex: The lizard's natural camouflage makes it hard to see.

camouflaged.def: 偽裝的、隱蔽的的人 (或物)
camouflaged.ex: The soldiers were well camouflaged in the deep forest.

camp.def: 營地、帳篷、兵營、紮營、平庸的、營地的人的人、紮營地 (營)
camp.col: summer camp, boot camp, concentration camp
camp.ex: We set up our camp near a small mountain stream.

campaign.def: 運動、戰役、活動、參加運動、運動家的人物、運動地 (運動)
campaign.col: election campaign, advertising campaign, smear campaign (抹黑行動)
campaign.ex: She led a successful campaign to save the local park.

campaigner.ex: The veteran rights campaigner addressed the large crowd.

campaigning.def: 參加競選、從事活動的人 (或物) (運)
campaigning.ex: Much of her time is spent campaigning for environmental issues.

camper.ex: I'm a happy camper now that I've found my keys.

campfire.ex: We sat around the campfire and sang songs all night.

camping.def: 露營、野營的的人 (或物) (營)
camping.ex: We are going camping in the mountains next weekend.

campos.ex: The vast campos provide grazing for thousands of cattle.

campus.ex: Most first-year students live on campus in the dorms.

camshaft.ex: The mechanic had to replace the worn engine camshaft.

can.def: 能、會、可以、(美)罐頭、(俚)監獄、裝罐、罐頭的的人物、罐頭地 (肯)
can.col: garbage can, watering can, tin can, opening can of worms (招惹麻煩)
can.ex: Can you help me with this heavy box?

can-can/cancan.ex: The show ended with a high-energy performance of the can-can.

canal.def: 運河、渠道、(解)管、開運河的人、開渠地 (渠)
canal.ex: The ship navigated through the narrow canal with ease.

canalization.ex: The river canalization improved irrigation in the valley.

canalize.def: 渠化、為...開運河、引導的人的人、渠化地 (導)
canalize.ex: He tried to canalize his energy into his new project.
